    Home › Categories › Container ships

    Container ships

    The box movers — from 58 containers in 1956 to 24,346 today.

    Ships here14
    Longest400.00 m
    Deliveries1956–2023
    Still in service13
    What it means

    The category, explained

    Container ships carry the standardised steel boxes that make modern trade possible, and they are measured in TEU — twenty-foot equivalent units, the count of standard boxes a ship is rated to carry. The growth curve is the story of globalisation itself: Ideal X carried 58 boxes in 1956; MSC Irina is rated for 24,346.

    The giants have converged on a hard envelope — about 400 metres long and 61.5 wide, the practical ceiling set by the Suez Canal, berth lengths and crane reach — so the record now moves by dozens of TEU rather than thousands. Every ship here is ranked by TEU, within its own unit, never against tankers or cruise ships.

Port safety Ships in storm The Silhouette Quiz Methodology Matchups Icon vs Titanic Legend vs Icon Titanic vs Queen Mary 2 Seawise Giant vs Icon Ever Given vs Ever Alot Emma Mærsk vs MSC Irina Blog The six days that stopped world trade Why the biggest ships stopped growing
    About the data. Every specification is compiled from operator, builder and registry sources and cited per row. Fleet and status figures are snapshots, marked ≈ where approximate and dated; individually verified figures carry a ✓. Ranks never mix units — cruise ships rank by GT, container ships by TEU, tankers by DWT. All silhouettes are original works of ships.fyi, drawn to true scale. Guides may contain clearly marked affiliate links; they never change what we recommend. Corrections and enquiries: business@luck.fyi — we fix errors visibly, not silently.
    © 2026 ships.fyi
    RecordsExplained
